meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no marketing skills (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no marketing skills. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to use project management to overcome no marketing skills. A guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: If you lack marketing skills, stop trying to 'sell' and start building a project management system that tracks user feedback cycles. By treating your product as a data-driven prototype, you build features users actually crave, turning your early adopters into your growth engine through systematic iteration and transparent development.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks perfecting your codebase, but your repository sits in silence. The cold reality of the 'No Marketing' gap is the graveyard of a thousand brilliant student projects. You feel the weight of a great product that no one sees, and the frustration of not knowing how to bridge the gap between 'Hello World' and 'First User'.

But here is the secret: you don't need a marketing degree; you need a system. By applying rigorous project management to your build, you transform your development process into a content engine. We are going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn your technical progress into a public-facing roadmap that markets itself, proving your value to the industry.

steps.title: Architecting the Product Roadmap
steps.description: Define your MVP features in a structured format so you can share your progress as a public-facing roadmap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a structured project roadmap schema for a SaaS tool that tracks user feedback directly from GitHub issues.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a project roadmap JSON schema based on the current codebase scope'

steps.title: Automating Progress Updates
steps.description: Use scripts to convert commit logs into digestible 'dev-logs' for your audience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a script that parses commit messages and creates a markdown summary of technical achievements for a weekly update.

steps.title: Implementing Feedback Loops
steps.description: Integrate a simple feedback mechanism into your UI that triggers a ticket in your project management board.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a lightweight feedback component that pushes user input directly to a project management API.

steps.title: Data-Driven Feature Prioritization
steps.description: Analyze your project management dashboard to decide what to build next based on user engagement, not guesswork.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project management board data and suggest the next three high-impact features to build based on current issue frequency.

steps.title: Public Launch Simulation
steps.description: Prepare your documentation and public roadmap for a 'soft launch' to attract your first beta testers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a compelling 'Public Roadmap' README page that highlights the problem solved and the current build progress.

pro_tips: Treat every commit as a marketing asset: write messages that explain the 'why' to a potential user, not just the 'what'.
Build a 'Public Roadmap' page early; it creates a psychological contract with users that encourages them to follow your journey.
Use your project management dashboard as a content calendar; your next blog post should always be about the feature you just finished.
Automate the transition from 'Issue Closed' to 'Feature Announced' in your social feeds to keep your audience engaged.
Focus on shipping small, incremental updates rather than waiting for a 'perfect' launch; consistent progress is the best marketing.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, deploy your roadmap to a live URL (like Vercel or Netlify) and share the link in one developer community where your target users hang out.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to turn your coding hours into a visible, marketable product. Stop hiding behind your IDE and start building in public. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
